why: Legacy 5-segment form: prefix+principal preserved, the subcapability 'typescript' dropped, the capability preserved, 'dead-letter' inserted (§5.2).

Stack-aware 6-segment form preserves the stack segment. §5.2 REQUIRES the TASKS_DEAD stream filters to match this form (grill D19 — the normative filter-alignment rule); the deployed legacy filter local.*.tasks.dead-letter.> does NOT (the * binds 'acme', then literal 'tasks' fails against 'default') — a conformance defect against D19, no longer an open decision.
The federated prefix mirrors the local grammar identically (§5.2).
Idempotency: re-deriving an already-dead-letter subject is a no-op, so re-routing an already-escalated task cannot double-nest the reserved segment (§5.2).
A subject with no 'tasks' segment has no dead-letter derivation; the deriver throws rather than fabricate one (§5.2).
